summary: Ukrainian authorities report a deadly rocket strike in Kryvyi Rih and intensive Russian attacks across Zaporizhzhia, with over a thousand strikes on 56 settlements. UAV and missile sightings proliferate from multiple fronts, including MiG‑31K launches carrying Kinzhal missiles. Ukrainian officials warn of a large‑scale offensive on several axes, citing heightened activity in Lyman, Kupiansk and the South‑Slobozhansky direction.
key_points: Three civilians killed and 23 injured by a Russian rocket attack on Kryvyi Rih (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Zaporizhzhia region suffered 1,050 strikes on 56 settlements, including 18 aviation attacks and 769 UAVs (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
Ukrainian radar channels report multiple Shahed drones approaching Kyiv and Vyshhorod from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
MiG‑31K fighter launched from Savasleyka carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, prompting nationwide air alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026; @eRadarrua/97913).
Ukrainian officials warn of a large‑scale Russian offensive on the Lyman, Kupiansk and South‑Slobozhansky axes (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
UAVs detected near Bliznyuky (Kharkiv), Krasnokutsk (Kharkiv) and over the Black Sea heading toward Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/66093; @kpszsu/66068; @kpszsu/66095).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and increased activity on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66083; @kpszsu/66102).
Russian “Lancet” loitering munitions attacked transport between Izium and Oskol in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian authorities report a deadly rocket strike in Kryvyi Rih and intensive Russian attacks across Zaporizhzhia, with over a thousand strikes on 56 settlements. UAV and missile sightings proliferate from multiple fronts, including MiG‑31K launches carrying Kinzhal missiles. Ukrainian officials warn of a large‑scale offensive on several axes, citing heightened activity in Lyman, Kupiansk and the South‑Slobozhansky direction.

points: The National Police Ombudsman rescued six men, including one with broken ribs, from a Mykolaiv regional detention centre after 18 days without contact (Source: @UkraineNow/82137).
In Kryvyi Rih, a Russian rocket strike killed three civilians and injured 23, with one victim in critical condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).

Zaporizhzhia region endured 1,050 attacks on 56 settlements, comprising 18 aviation strikes, 769 UAVs, three MLRS salvos and 260 artillery strikes, causing injuries and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A truck parking lot in Zaporizhzhia was hit during the night, according to local reports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31098).
Shelling in Kherson’s Velyky Kopan (Aleshky district) wounded a civilian man (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31106).
Ukrainian officials warned of a large‑scale Russian offensive affecting the Lyman, Kupiansk and South‑Slobozhansky directions (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
Ukrainian radar channels reported a Shahed drone from the north targeting Kyiv and Vyshhorod (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
MiG‑31K fighter launched from Savasleyka carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, prompting nationwide air‑raid alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026; @eRadarrua/97913).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb strikes on Donetsk, intensifying the eastern front combat (Source: @kpszsu/66083).
Russian forces increased missile activity nationwide, with alerts of MiG‑31K launches carrying Kinzhal missiles (Source: @kpszsu/66103).
Threats of ballistic weapon use were noted from the Millerovo direction (Source: @kpszsu/66084).
Russian “Lancet” loitering munitions attacked transport routes between Izium and Oskol in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
UAVs were observed operating from the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv region, indicating maritime launch capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/66095).
Multiple Shahed drones were launched from the north targeting Kyiv and surrounding areas (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
Russian officials claim to be ready for peace talks based on Istanbul agreements (Source: @tass_agency/382079).
President Putin announced the awarding of the Zhukov Order to the FSO Presidential Regiment (Source: @tass_agency/382094).
UAVs were sighted near Bliznyuky and Oleksandrivka in Kharkiv region, indicating continued aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66093).
A UAV passed north of Chernihiv heading toward Koryukivka, suggesting activity on the northern flank (Source: @kpszsu/66080).
UAVs from Sumy moved toward Poltava, targeting Kotelva and Opishnia, showing cross‑regional drone trajectories (Source: @kpszsu/66079).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the eastern direction, with increased aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66102).
A UAV near Krasnokutsk in Kharkiv headed south, possibly supporting ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/66068).
Russian “Lancet” loitering munitions attacked transport between Izium and Oskol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
Ukrainian forces reported a rocket strike in Vinnytsia towards Voronovytsia (Source: @kpszsu/66107).
Continued shelling in Kherson region, with civilian injuries reported in Velyky Kopan (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31106).
Shahed drones launched from the north targeted Kyiv and Vyshhorod, prompting air‑raid alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
MiG‑31K launch from Savasleyka with Kinzhal missile caused nationwide alarms (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026; @eRadarrua/97913).
UAVs detected north of Chernihiv heading to Koryukivka, indicating surveillance activity (Source: @kpszsu/66080).
Air‑raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 22:03 and 22:18, reflecting heightened threat levels (Source: @eRadarrua/97912; @eRadarrua/97915).
Ukrainian radar reported a Shahed drone approaching Kyiv from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67989).
Russian missile threat from Millerovo was highlighted, potentially affecting northern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/66084).
UAV activity over the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv may impact northern coastal defenses (Source: @kpszsu/66095).
Ukrainian officials warned of a large‑scale offensive affecting multiple fronts, including northern axes (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
UAVs from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Mykolaiv region, indicating southern maritime launch points (Source: @kpszsu/66095).
Russian rocket attacks in Kryvyi Rih caused three civilian deaths and 23 injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Zaporizhzhia region experienced 1,050 strikes on 56 settlements, with extensive UAV and artillery usage (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
A rocket strike in Vinnytsia targeted Voronovytsia, showing southern operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/66107).
Ukrainian officials cited heightened activity on the South‑Slobozhansky direction as part of a broader offensive (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Donetsk, affecting southern operational depth (Source: @kpszsu/66083).
UAV sightings near Krasnokutsk (Kharkiv) heading south may support southern maneuvers (Source: @kpszsu/66068).
Biological terror claim of Russian activity spreading Siberian plague in Kherson region (Source: @UkraineNow/82139).
MiG‑31K fighter launched from Savasleyka carrying an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile, triggering nationwide air alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026; @eRadarrua/97913).
Multiple Shahed drones were detected approaching Kyiv from the north, prompting local air‑raid warnings (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb strikes on Donetsk, intensifying air operations (Source: @kpszsu/66083).
UAV activity reported across Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Sumy and the Black Sea, indicating widespread aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66093; @kpszsu/66079; @kpszsu/66080; @kpszsu/66095).
Air‑raid alerts were issued over Kyiv at 22:03 and 22:18, reflecting heightened missile and drone threats (Source: @eRadarrua/97912; @eRadarrua/97915).
Russian “Lancet” loitering munitions engaged transport routes in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
Threat of ballistic weapons from Millerovo was highlighted, suggesting potential long‑range missile use (Source: @kpszsu/66084).
Ukrainian radar channels reported increased activity of enemy tactical aviation on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66102).

key_developments: Three civilians killed and 23 injured by a Russian rocket strike on Kryvyi Rih, highlighting the continued threat to urban centers (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Zaporizhzhia region suffered 1,050 strikes on 56 settlements, including 18 aviation attacks and 769 UAVs, indicating a high‑intensity campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/82126).
MiG‑31K launch from Savasleyka with an X‑47M2 Kinzhal missile prompted nationwide air‑raid alerts, showing Russia’s use of hypersonic weapons (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026; @eRadarrua/97913).
Ukrainian officials warned of a large‑scale Russian offensive on the Lyman, Kupiansk and South‑Slobozhansky axes, suggesting preparation for coordinated assaults (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).

UAVs detected near Bliznyuky (Kharkiv), Krasnokutsk (Kharkiv) and over the Black Sea heading toward Mykolaiv, indicating multi‑directional drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/66093; @kpszsu/66068; @kpszsu/66095).
Shahed drones launched from the north targeted Kyiv and Vyshhorod, causing multiple air‑raid alerts and demonstrating persistent low‑altitude threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67990).
Russian “Lancet” loitering munitions attacked transport between Izium and Oskol, disrupting logistics in the Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31104).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are employing a mixed arsenal of rockets, UAVs, guided aerial bombs and hypersonic missiles, creating a multi‑layered threat across all fronts. The concentration of attacks on civilian infrastructure in Kryvyi Rih and Zaporizhzhia, combined with the announced large‑scale offensive, suggests preparation for coordinated ground operations. Continued drone activity from maritime and inland launch points expands the battlefield, while the use of Kinzhal‑armed MiG‑31K indicates a willingness to employ strategic strike capabilities, raising the risk of rapid escalation.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have intensified civil defense measures, issuing frequent air‑raid alerts and mobilizing rescue efforts in heavily hit cities. Regional commanders report increased artillery and air defense deployments, particularly around Kyiv, Kharkiv and the southern oblasts. International partners are monitoring the hypersonic missile use, with diplomatic statements urging restraint, while aid channels focus on medical and reconstruction support for the affected populations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring continues to track UAV flight paths, revealing cross‑regional trajectories that may precede ground maneuvers. The emergence of ballistic threats from Millerovo and the reported biological terror activities in Kherson warrant heightened bio‑security and missile defense readiness. Signals intelligence indicates possible coordination between Russian tactical aviation and ground forces, underscoring the need for integrated air‑ground situational awareness.
